potential_technical_flaws.flaw_title: Lack of HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) Header
potential_technical_flaws.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) ensures that a user's browser only connects to a website over HTTPS. This can help prevent man-in-the-middle attacks and cookie hijacking.
potential_technical_flaws.fix_law: Implement HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) by adding the following response header: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload

potential_technical_flaws.description: XSS Protection Header can help prevent cross-site scripting attacks by instructing the browser to block or sanitize suspicious content.
potential_technical_flaws.fix_law: Enable XSS Protection by adding the following response header: X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block

potential_technical_flaws.description: Content Security Policy (CSP) helps prevent various types of attacks like XSS by defining the sources from which resources can be loaded on a webpage.
potential_technical_flaws.fix_law: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) by adding the following response header: Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'self'

potential_technical_flaws.description: Open Redirect Vulnerability occurs when a website allows external links to redirect to any URL, opening the possibility for phishing attacks or other malicious activities.
potential_technical_flaws.fix_law: Validate and sanitize all user-input or redirect URLs to ensure they only redirect to trusted domains.

potential_technical_flaws.description: X-Frame-Options can prevent clickjacking attacks by controlling whether or not a browser should be allowed to render a page in a frame, iframe, or object element.
potential_technical_flaws.fix_law: Prevent clickjacking attacks by adding the X-Frame-Options header with the value 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN'

vulnerabilities.fix: Implement proper security headers like Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and Strict-Transport-Security to enhance the security of the website.
Sanitize user inputs, encode output data, and implement Content Security Policy (CSP) to prevent XSS attacks.
Implement X-Frame-Options header with 'deny' or 'sameorigin' directive to prevent clickjacking attacks.
Set secure flag for cookies, enable HttpOnly flag, and implement SameSite attribute to prevent CSRF and session hijacking attacks.
Regularly update and patch all software components including web server, database, and other third-party libraries to the latest secure versions.
Add security headers such as Content-Security-Policy (CSP), X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) to enhance security.
Implement input validation and output encoding to sanitize user input and prevent any malicious scripts from being executed.
Add HTTP security headers such as Referrer-Policy and Feature-Policy to protect against various attacks and enhance security.
Set secure and HttpOnly attributes for cookies to prevent them from being accessed by malicious scripts and transmitted over unencrypted connections.
Regularly monitor and assess third-party integrations for security vulnerabilities, and ensure they meet security best practices to prevent any potential risks.

findings.description: The website is using outdated versions of software which may contain known security vulnerabilities.
findings.fix-law: Update all software to the latest versions to patch any known security vulnerabilities.

findings.description: The website is missing important HTTP security headers like Content Security Policy (CSP), HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), and X-Frame-Options.
findings.fix-law: Implement necessary HTTP security headers like Content Security Policy (CSP), HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS), and X-Frame-Options to enhance website security.

findings.description: The website is revealing server details like server type and version which can be exploited by attackers.
findings.fix-law: Disable server information disclosure to prevent attackers from gathering information about the server.

findings.description: The website does not have protections in place to mitigate cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.
findings.fix-law: Implement proper input validation and output encoding to prevent cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities.

findings.description: The website is not setting secure flags on cookies, leaving them vulnerable to interception by attackers over an insecure network.
findings.fix-law: Set the secure flag on all cookies to ensure they are only sent over secure (HTTPS) connections.

fix-law.implementation: Add security HTTP headers to the web server configuration.
fix-law.example: Implement headers like Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, and Content-Security-Policy.

fix-law.implementation: Regularly update all software components including web server, CMS, plugins, and libraries.
fix-law.example: Enable automatic updates or establish a routine to check for and apply updates.

fix-law.implementation: Secure sensitive files and directories, avoid displaying detailed error messages, and restrict access to critical information.
fix-law.example: Implement proper access control mechanisms and disable directory listing.

fix-law.implementation: Validate and sanitize user input, encode output, and use security mechanisms provided by frameworks or libraries.
fix-law.example: Implement input validation, output encoding, and Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate XSS attacks.

fix-law.implementation: Include anti-CSRF tokens in forms and validate them on the server side before processing requests.
fix-law.example: Generate unique tokens for each user session and verify token validity for each form submission.
